description_long: This object is an orange carrot root with a slightly irregular, cylindrical shape and a blunt, rounded tip. The surface is textured with shallow grooves and subtle ridges, showing natural imperfections and a matte finish. The coloration is predominantly orange with some lighter patches and faint white streaks, typical of a freshly harvested carrot. The top end displays a rough, brownish area where the leafy greens were once attached, while the bottom end is rounded and slightly tapered. There are no visible green leaves or secondary roots branching from the main body. The carrot appears solid and dense, with no visible signs of dirt or soil on its surface.
description: A solid, orange carrot root with a rough, matte surface and a blunt, rounded tip. The top shows a brownish area where leaves were attached, and the body is free of dirt or branching.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the carrot, highlighting its cylindrical shape and textured orange surface.
description_view_1: Top-down view focusing on the brownish, rough area where the leafy greens were attached.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the carrot's length and surface texture.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view showing the blunt, rounded tip of the carrot and the subtle tapering at the end.
